title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Six: Which shows how Abdullah went from the frying pan into the fire (Fragment 3)
text:
The dog had its nose rammed into the corner. It was not attending. Abdullah heard, unmistakable even through the thick walls of the dungeon, the tramp of feet and the rattle of keys. Someone was coming. He gave up persuading the dog. He lay flat on the carpet.

“Here, boy!” he said. “Come and lick my face!”

The dog understood that. It left the corner, jumped on Abdullah’s chest, and proceeded to obey him.

“Carpet,” Abdullah whispered from under the busy tongue. “To the Bazaar, but do not land. Hover beside Jamal’s stall.”

The carpet rose and rushed sideways—which was just as well. Keys were unlocking the dungeon door. Abdullah was not any too sure how the carpet left the dungeon because the dog was still licking his face and he was forced to keep his eyes shut. He felt a dank shadow pass across him—perhaps that was when they melted through the wall—and then bright sunlight. The dog lifted its head into the sunlight, puzzled. Abdullah squinted sideways across his chains and saw a high wall rear in front of them and then fall below as the carpet rose smoothly over it. Then came a succession of towers and roofs, quite familiar to Abdullah though he had only seen them by night before. And after that the carpet went planing down toward the outer edge of the Bazaar. For the palace of the Sultan was indeed only five minutes’ walk from Abdullah’s booth.

Jamal’s stall came into view, and beside it, Abdullah’s own wrecked booth, with carpets flung all over the walkway. Obviously soldiers had searched there for Flower-in-the-Night. Jamal was dozing, with his head on his arms, between a big simmering pot of squid and a charcoal grill with skewered meat smoking on it. He raised his head, and his one eye stared as the carpet came to hang in the air in front of him.

“Down, boy!” Abdullah said. “Jamal, call your dog.”

Jamal was clearly very scared. It is no fun keeping the stall next door to anyone a sultan wishes to impale on a stake. He seemed speechless. Since the dog was taking no notice, either, Abdullah struggled into sitting position, clanking, rattling, and sweating. This tipped the dog off. It jumped nimbly to the stall counter, where Jamal absently seized it in his arms.

“What do you want me to do?” he asked, eyeing the chains. “Shall I fetch a blacksmith?”

Abdullah was touched at this proof of Jamal’s friendship. But sitting up had given him a view down the walkway between the stalls. He could see the soles of running feet down there and flying garments. It seemed that one boothkeeper was on his way to fetch the Watch, though there was something about the running figure that reminded Abdullah rather strongly of Assif. “No,” he said. “There’s no time.” Clanking, he wriggled his left leg over the edge of the carpet. “Do this for me instead. Put your hand on the embroidery above my left boot.”

Jamal obediently stretched out a brawny arm and, very gingerly, touched the embroidery. “Is it a spell?” he asked nervously.

“No,” said Abdullah. “It’s a hidden purse. Put your hand in and take the money out of it.”

Jamal was puzzled, but his fingers groped, found the way into the purse, and came out as a fistful of gold. “There’s a fortune here,” he said. “Will this buy your freedom?”

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Six: Which shows how Abdullah went from the frying pan into the fire

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows a lack of attention or involvement.
vocabulary.example: Jamal absently seized the dog in his arms.

vocabulary.definition: A black substance made by burning wood slowly in little oxygen, used for fuel or drawing.
vocabulary.example: A charcoal grill with skewered meat was smoking on it.

vocabulary.definition: Making a loud, sharp ringing sound, typically of metal hitting metal.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah was clanking and rattling as he sat up.

vocabulary.definition: Unpleasantly moist or damp, often with a musty smell.
vocabulary.example: He felt a dank shadow pass across him.

vocabulary.definition: A dark underground prison, especially in a castle.
vocabulary.example: Even through the thick walls of the dungeon, the tramp of feet was heard.

vocabulary.definition: The amount that a closed hand can hold.
vocabulary.example: Jamal took out a fistful of gold from the hidden purse.

vocabulary.definition: A very large amount of money or assets.
vocabulary.example: There’s a fortune here, enough to buy freedom.

vocabulary.definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants without hindrance or restraint.
vocabulary.example: Will this buy your freedom?

vocabulary.definition: In a careful or cautious manner.
vocabulary.example: He very gingerly touched the embroidery.

vocabulary.definition: A small concealed bag or container for money.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah explained it was a hidden purse with money.

vocabulary.definition: To remain in one place in the air.
vocabulary.example: The carpet was told to hover beside Jamal’s stall.

vocabulary.definition: To pierce with a sharp point or stake, usually causing injury or death.
vocabulary.example: It is no fun keeping the stall next door to anyone a sultan wishes to impale on a stake.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows willingness to obey.
vocabulary.example: Jamal obediently stretched out his arm.

vocabulary.definition: To begin or continue a course of action.
vocabulary.example: The dog jumped on Abdullah’s chest and proceeded to obey him.

vocabulary.definition: A rapid succession of short sharp noises.
vocabulary.example: The rattle of keys could be heard at the door.

vocabulary.definition: To move quickly and with urgency.
vocabulary.example: The carpet rose and rushed sideways.

vocabulary.definition: To take hold of suddenly and forcibly.
vocabulary.example: Jamal absently seized the dog in his arms.

vocabulary.definition: Pierced or threaded on a skewer for cooking or serving.
vocabulary.example: Meat smoking on the charcoal grill was skewered.

vocabulary.definition: Unable to speak, usually because of shock or surprise.
vocabulary.example: Jamal seemed speechless when the carpet appeared.

vocabulary.definition: A form of words supposed to have magic power.
vocabulary.example: Jamal asked nervously if it was a spell.

vocabulary.definition: To look with eyes partly closed, usually to see better.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah squinted sideways across his chains.

vocabulary.definition: A number of things that follow each other in order.
vocabulary.example: Then came a succession of towers and roofs.

vocabulary.definition: To give someone a warning or secret information.
vocabulary.example: This tipped the dog off, and it jumped to the stall counter.

vocabulary.definition: A heavy step or the sound this makes.
vocabulary.example: The tramp of feet echoed down the corridor.

vocabulary.definition: Clear and cannot be confused with anything else.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah heard, unmistakable even through the thick walls of the dungeon, the tramp of feet and the rattle of keys.
